title: ‘Scapegoating’: Sports department denies costing Jeffreys Bay World Surf League slot
description: SURFING NEWS - The Department of Sport, Arts...
bodyText: [{"type":"intro_bold","text":"World Surf Tour CEO Ryan Crosby described Jeffreys Bay as 'one of the best waves in the world' but Government cited application regulations."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"SURFING NEWS - The Department of Sport, Arts and Culture (DSAC) has distanced itself from South Africa’s failure to secure the hosting of a premier international event.","html":"SURFING NEWS - The Department of Sport, Arts and Culture (DSAC) has distanced itself from South Africa’s failure to secure the hosting of a premier international event."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The World Surfing League (WSL) pulled Jeffreys Bay from its 2026 championship tour schedule this week due to a lack of financial support."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"This year marks the 50th anniversary of the international surfing tour, but the department tasked with safeguarding the nation’s sporting interests claims procedural regulations were not followed.","html":"This year marks the 50th anniversary of the international surfing tour, but the department tasked with safeguarding the nation’s sporting interests claims procedural regulations were not followed."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Africa’s surfing mecca had hosted the event for over three decades and was an economic staple for the small coastal town.","html":"Africa’s surfing mecca had hosted the event for over three decades and was an economic staple for the small coastal town."},{"type":"heading","text":"‘Best waves in the world’","level":"h2"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"In announcing its 2026 calendar this weekend, the WSL replaced Jeffreys Bay with a picturesque holiday destination outside Hamilton on New Zealand’s North Island.","html":"In announcing its 2026 calendar this weekend, the WSL replaced Jeffreys Bay with a picturesque holiday destination outside Hamilton on New Zealand’s North Island."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"WSL CEO Ryan Crosby expressed his disappointment at moving the event but hoped it would return in future.","html":"WSL CEO Ryan Crosby expressed his disappointment at moving the event but hoped it would return in future."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"“We love J-Bay and the community there, and we’re really grateful to them for welcoming us for so many years. It’s undoubtedly one of the best waves in the world,” said Crosby."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“We did our very best to make J-Bay work in 2025, but the financial support isn’t there to make it viable this year,” he explained.","html":"“We did our very best to make J-Bay work in 2025, but the financial support isn’t there to make it viable this year,” he explained."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Democratic Alliance stated the failure to secure the surfing crown jewel was a blow to Eastern Cape tourism and employment in the area.","html":"The Democratic Alliance stated the failure to secure the surfing crown jewel was a blow to Eastern Cape tourism and employment in the area."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“Behind every cancelled booking and every quiet shop floor is a family whose income depended on this event. Government inaction should not be paid for by ordinary residents,” stated member of the Eastern Cape legislature Vicky Knoetze.","html":"“Behind every cancelled booking and every quiet shop floor is a family whose income depended on this event. Government inaction should not be paid for by ordinary residents,” stated member of the Eastern Cape legislature Vicky Knoetze."},{"type":"heading","text":"‘Form of deflection’","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Knoetze stated that the department and Minister Gayton McKenzie had failed to confirm funding for the event, while the Kouga municipality and sponsors had pledged their support.","html":"Knoetze stated that the department and Minister Gayton McKenzie had failed to confirm funding for the event, while the Kouga municipality and sponsors had pledged their support."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“Major international sporting events play a critical role in attracting visitors, stimulating local economies, and showcasing the Eastern Cape on the global stage. They require strategic leadership, not silence,” she said.","html":"“Major international sporting events play a critical role in attracting visitors, stimulating local economies, and showcasing the Eastern Cape on the global stage. They require strategic leadership, not silence,” she said."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"DSAC stated on Tuesday that the surfing competition was privately owned and that previous editions had received little government involvement or funding.","html":"DSAC stated on Tuesday that the surfing competition was privately owned and that previous editions had received little government involvement or funding."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The department explained the procedures for submitting hosting applications for international events, stating that approvals were within the minister’s prerogative but also subject to Cabinet scrutiny.","html":"The department explained the procedures for submitting hosting applications for international events, stating that approvals were within the minister’s prerogative but also subject to Cabinet scrutiny."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"“In the case of the WSL event, the department and ministry did not receive any formal application submitted in accordance with the regulations."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“As no request for national government funding, guarantees or associated services was formally submitted for consideration, no funding decision was therefore taken at the national level.","html":"“As no request for national government funding, guarantees or associated services was formally submitted for consideration, no funding decision was therefore taken at the national level."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“All public comments that are now seeking to place blame at the feet of the minister for the inability to secure a WSL event for 2026 are misguided, a form of deflection and unjustified scapegoating,” the department concluded.","html":"“All public comments that are now seeking to place blame at the feet of the minister for the inability to secure a WSL event for 2026 are misguided, a form of deflection and unjustified scapegoating,” the department concluded."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Article: Caxton publication, The Citizen","html":"Article: Caxton publication, The Citizen"}]

images.alt: ‘Scapegoating’: Sports department denies costing Jeffreys Bay World Surf League slot

author: The Citizen
teaser: World Surf Tour CEO Ryan Crosby described Jeffreys Bay as 'one of the best waves in the world' but Government cited application regulations.
featuredCaption: Sarah Baum of South Africa in action at the Corona Cero Open J-Bay in July last year. Photo: Kody McGregor/World Surf League
